label: The Model Home (Sudden Valley)
notes: Show-home of the abandoned Sudden Valley development; the family's default residence. Half-built neighbors, falling cabinetry, attic hideout for George Sr. (s02).

label: Balboa Towers penthouse
notes: Lucille's penthouse; Lucille Austero lives in the same building. Buster's home for most of his life.

label: Bluth's Original Frozen Banana Stand
notes: The family's first business (1953). Burned down by George Michael and Michael (s01e02 Top Banana) — unfortunately there was always money in the banana stand: $250,000 lining its walls.

label: Bluth Company offices
notes: Real-estate development firm; the boardroom, Michael's office, and the banana-yellow staircar fleet base.

label: Orange County Prison
notes: George Sr.'s home for most of s01 ('I'm doing the time of my life!'). Site of family visits, Tobias's method-acting incarceration, and the White Power Bill incident.

label: Wee Britain
notes: British-themed district; home of Rita Leeds and the Mr. F arc (s03). Poppy-seed vendors and a tiny town aesthetic.

label: The Bluth family cabin
notes: Site of generations of broken camping promises (The Cabin Show, s03e01); later towed on a flatbed.

label: Mexico (various)
notes: George Sr.'s flight destination (s02 ¡Amigos!), the Mongolian-barbecue mix-up, and the s05 'self-deportation' arc.

label: The Queen Mary
notes: Club/venue ship; stolen by Lucille in the s03 finale (Development Arrested) during the retirement-party escape.
